In LTL, to express 'a property does not hold for more than n steps at a time', we essentially specify that whenever the bad condition starts, it cannot last beyond n consecutive steps. One standard pattern is: Globally, if the bad condition holds now and continuously for n steps, then it must not hold at the (n+1)th step: G((b ∧ X b ∧ X X b ... (n times)) -> X^n !b).
